Philosophy: Light and Shadow provides absolute sandbox freedom, where quests and contracts coexist as parallel, optional activities that support macro progression, world building, and repeatable endgame loops without imposing mandatory theme-park progression lanes.

CanonicalRules.Text: The game uses both persistent quest content and repeatable contract content as parallel world activity systems.
Quests and contracts are optional world activities and never impose mandatory linear progression paths.
Quests are abundant world activities and are not limited to progression gating. Players may pursue quests for progression, lore, rewards, exploration, or personal goals.
Contracts are repeatable tasks that provide long-term gameplay loops independent of story progression.
Quest rewards may include progression, economy, knowledge, and unlock-based rewards.
Hunts remain the primary source of character experience while quests provide secondary progression rewards.
NPC factions function as progression, learning, and contract hubs rather than player social organizations.
Spells are learned through both faction training and quest-based unlocks depending on spell rarity and narrative significance.
Quests never fail permanently and remain recoverable regardless of interruption or abandonment.
Quest systems must support sandbox freedom and may never replace open-world activity choice as the core gameplay philosophy.
NPC factions are not limited to a fixed number and may expand dynamically as the world evolves through future content, continents, and narrative systems.
Quest rewards use deterministic reward templates selected from fixed canonical reward pools.

MAIN.Purpose: Macro progression, major world unlocks, continent access, and primary story arcs.

STORY.Purpose: Lore, worldbuilding, history of Primordials, faction stories, and deep historic conflicts.
STORY.Gating: No progression gating. Fully optional narrative exploration.

UNLOCK.Purpose: Unlock spells, crafting recipes, progression systems, or specialty hunts.
UNLOCK.Style: Serves as progression milestones that players pursue at their own choice.

SIDE.Purpose: Enrich local village depth, support minor NPC narratives, and provide optional rewards.
SIDE.Abundance: Highly abundant throughout all game regions.

ContractSystem.Repeatability: Infinite repeatable world loops. Refreshes periodically or on completion.

Types.Target: Standard monster targets in specific regions.
Named rare/elite monsters with dynamic spawn indicators.
Protect regional caravans or emissaries traveling through non-safe zones.
Transport fragile cargo/messages between distant outposts under open-world conditions.

RewardsEngine.XPPriorityLaw: Hunts remain the primary source of character experience. Quests and contracts provide smaller experience gains to preserve hunt-centric progression balance.

NPCFactions.Architecture: Extensible. Factions are not limited to a fixed number and may expand dynamically through future content.

StarterList.Name: Hunter Lodges
StarterList.Description: Hunters and tracker circles providing hunt contracts, tracking training, and specialized hunting gear templates.

StarterList.Name: Mage Orders
StarterList.Description: Arcane researchers and elemental masters offering spell learning, spell quests, and ancient lore.

StarterList.Name: Religious Orders
StarterList.Description: Temple keepers guarding holy altars, offering blessing services, and quest contracts related to the Primordials.

StarterList.Name: Mercenary Guilds
StarterList.Description: Solder-of-fortune guilds offering bounties, delivery, and escort contracts.

StarterList.Name: Shadow Cults
StarterList.Description: Hidden cults operating outside safe zones, providing high-risk contracts, forbidden spell unlocks, and chaos lore.

SpellLearningSystem.FactionTrainerSpells: Common discipline spells available directly from faction NPC trainers in exchange for coin and elemental level prerequisites.
SpellLearningSystem.QuestUnlockedSpells: Rare, unique, forbidden, or high-lore spells unlocked only after completing specific Main, Story, or Unlock quests.

FailurePolicy.NPCDonorImmortality: All quest-giver and quest-target NPCs are fully immortal. They can never die, preventing any possibility of progression deadlocks.
